steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: Lowering the voting age to 18 directly caused young people to gain voting opportunities. The second sentence is the outcome of the first.

steps.title: Match the transition to the relationship
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect. The blank needs a transition signaling that young people gaining the vote is a direct result of lowering the voting age.
  • Choice A — "Similarly,": signals a parallel between two comparable situations, but only one situation is described here, so fails. ✗
  • Choice B — "By contrast,": signals opposition between two ideas, but the second sentence follows from the first rather than opposing it, so fails. ✗
  • Choice C — "Consequently,": signals that one event results directly from another, capturing the causal link, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "Nevertheless,": signals an unexpected result despite an obstacle, but nothing is working against the outcome here, so fails. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
